prompt:
Payout onboarding keeps eating bad bank details: support pastes IBANs out of emails with spaces, hyphens, lowercase, the odd asterisk from a redaction tool, and today our 'validation' is len(s) > 10. Then the bank bounces the transfer three days later and the customer screams. I want a proper module, iban.py, stdlib only, that our intake form and the nightly batch importer can both use.

Supported countries and their shapes (n=digits, a=letters, c=letters-or-digits; length includes the CCkk prefix): AT 20 (16n), BE 16 (12n), CH 21 (5n 12c), DE 22 (18n), ES 24 (20n), FR 27 (10n 11c 2n), GB 22 (4a 14n), IT 27 (1a 10n 12c), NL 18 (4a 10n), PL 28 (24n). Keep the table data-driven — we'll add countries.

validate(s) -> canonical electronic IBAN (uppercase, zero separators) or raises IBANError (make it a ValueError subclass — existing call sites catch that). Spaces and hyphens anywhere are separators, case is forgiven. Everything else is checked in THIS order, because the batch report reasons must be stable: any character that isn't A-Z/0-9 after cleanup -> error naming the character; fewer than 4 chars -> error saying it's too short; first two chars not letters, or letters we don't support -> error mentioning the country (and echoing the code when there is one); chars 3-4 not digits -> error mentioning the check digits; wrong total length for the country -> error stating the expected length; BBAN not matching the country's structure -> error mentioning structure; and finally the real mod-97 check (rearrange, A=10..Z=35, remainder must be 1) -> error mentioning the checksum. is_valid(s) -> bool, never raises.

format_electronic(s) and format_paper(s): both validate first (garbage in, IBANError out). Paper = groups of 4 separated by single spaces, last group short if it must be. bban(s) -> the part after the CCkk prefix, validated first.

with_check_digits(country, bban) -> full IBAN with the two check digits computed (the mod-97 = 1 arrangement), zero-padded to two chars — 8 comes out as '08'. Validate the country and the BBAN's length/structure before computing; case-insensitive on both args.

validate_batch(items) -> one result dict per input, same order, nothing raises: {'input': <original string untouched>, 'ok': True, 'iban': <canonical>} or {'input': ..., 'ok': False, 'error': <the reason message>} — no 'iban' key on failures, the importer's CSV writer chokes on ragged keys.

test_iban.py is the contract, run with python3 test_iban.py, don't edit it. The sample IBANs in there are the published test numbers from the banks, they're genuinely valid.
